sections.paragraph:
(“strong,” from אֶדְרָע), [Edrei], pr.n.-

(1) of the ancient metropolis of Bashan, situated in the territory allotted to the tribe of Manasseh, Num 21:33 Deu 1:4 Jos 12:4 called by Eusebius Ἀδραά, by Ptolemy Ἄδρα, by the Arabian geographers اذرعات, and now Drâa; see Relandi Palest. P. 547. [Edhra, Rob. app. p. 155.]

(2) of a town in the tribe of Naphtali, Jos 19:37.
